metadata.title: The Dollar Canon — AI+1 series 2/4
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929 all text; SPARK #D97757 the one accent; callout ink #161511 on cream. Do not retint.
metadata.greeting_note: Portuguese short form (HAI budget) — HAI takes only the shortest forms (Hi · Ola · Hej · Ciao); never spell out HumanitariansAI in the greeting (the footer chip does that). Wagwan n/a (Bear only).
metadata.purpose: CUSTOMER pitch, not investor pitch: sell custom workshops, custom courses, and $1 Kindle books from a nonprofit doing good, and send viewers to the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. SPARK-LINE LAW: every inner beat carries a short serif spark line. HANDOFF LAW: 2nd-to-last beat is the composer with a paste-ready action, greeting 'Your turn.'. OUTRO LAW: restate the title, poster serif, terracotta period. House grammar: >=1 PREDICT beat; verdict page carries the phase gate; decisions.json + DECISIONS.md ship beside this sheet.

beats.narration_text: Yes — a dollar, on purpose. Here's why the canonical version of every course is priced like a coffee tip, and what you actually get for it.

props.command: is the textbook really a dollar?

beats.narration_text: One dollar on Kindle — free if you have Kindle Unlimited. Every course. And it's the full canonical textbook, not a teaser chapter with an upsell at the end.
The lectures are free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube — the complete course in video, public. Assign it to a class, binge it yourself, check our teaching before you spend anything.
The slide decks run on our site as voiced, animated pages — every deck narrated, every build animated, right in the browser. No download, no license key, no viewer to install.
So predict the business logic: why would we price the canon at a dollar, when the custom versions are what pay for the work? Take a second.

Because the canon is the audit. You can read the book, watch the course, and hear the decks before you ever email us — so when you buy a custom version, you know exactly what the base is. No demo call. And we're a five-oh-one-C-three: on the canon, reach matters more than margin.
One page. A dollar buys the audit: the full book, the full videos, the talking decks. Read the base before you ever pay for a version of it.

artifactLines: $1 Kindle textbook (free on Kindle Unlimited) — the full canonical text, every course.
The complete course in video, free on the Humanitarians AI YouTube.
Every slide deck voiced and animated on the site — nothing to install.
Phase gate: audit the canon first · buy custom only when the base has earned it.

beats.narration_text: Your turn — audit us. Paste this, pick a course, and check the book against the videos. If the canon holds up, the custom conversation is worth your time.

props.command: Pick one Humanitarians AI course. Pull its $1 Kindle textbook and its free YouTube playlist, and check the chapters against the videos — does the coverage match?

beats.narration_text: The dollar canon. Next: your version.
